title: Console Simulator
subtitle: Build an interactive demo page that replays claude agent flow build sessions with console output and flow diagrams
prompt: /plan I want to demonstrate what it is like to use the agent flow system by showing what happens in the console when I actually use it. I could do a real session and save the log, then have a GitHub Page that loads ?logid=xxxx showing a console simulator alongside a flow diagram of the agents. Speed controls, adversarial looping visible. A way to demo this to a group.

commands.plan: /plan I want to demonstrate what it is like to use the agent flow system by showing what happens in the console when I actually use it

events.text: STARTING /PLAN
Orchestrator active

Received user brief: build an interactive demo page that replays claude agent flow build sessions — console simulator alongside a flow diagram, with speed controls and adversarial loop visualisation. Deployable to GitHub Pages. Dispatching explorer and ideator in parallel.

Two parallel tasks: (1) explorer maps existing docs/public/ pages to understand the design system and patterns, (2) ideator generates creative approaches for console+flow visualisation. Both are independent.

events.args: agent=explorer, task="Map docs/public/. Glob all HTML files, read at least one sibling page in full. Extract: CSS custom properties, breadcrumb nav markup, footer markup, font faces, keyframe animation names. Report design system summary."

agent=ideator, task="Generate 3 visualisation approaches for replaying claude agent flow sessions: console output + agent flow diagram. Consider: (A) SVG-based animated diagram, (B) DOM-based vertical flow boxes, (C) timeline/swimlane view. For each: layout, interactivity model, trade-offs."

events.text: Explorer active

docs/public/index.html
docs/public/agent-orchestration-pipeline.html
docs/public/build-pipeline.html
docs/public/adversarial-loop.html
docs/public/wegovy-scorecard.html
... (12 pages total)

CSS :root: --opus/#7c3aed, --sonnet/#0071e3, --haiku/#28a745, --bg/#fbfbfd, --surface/#ffffff, --border/#e5e5ea. Font: SF Pro Display with -apple-system fallback. Breadcrumb nav with hamburger drawer for mobile. Inline style+script pattern, no external deps.

Vertical stacked box pattern: .flow-node with model-colour badges, status text, completed/active states. Adversarial loop section with dashed red border. DOM-based, not SVG. Connectors as thin vertical lines.

Design system report: 12 pages found. Consistent light theme with CSS tokens. All pages are single self-contained HTML files — inline CSS + JS, no build step, no external dependencies. Vertical flow box pattern already exists in build-pipeline.html.
Ideator active

Three visualisation approaches for replaying agent sessions. Need to consider: GitHub Pages constraint (static HTML only), existing design system, demo-friendly (presenter can talk over it), adversarial loop must be visually dramatic.

Approach A — SVG Flow Diagram: Animated SVG with nodes at fixed positions, stroke-dashoffset arrow animations. Pro: smooth animations, crisp at any zoom. Con: complex layout math, hard to show status text, small on mobile.

Approach B — DOM Vertical Stacked Boxes: Reuse existing build-pipeline.html pattern. Active node glows, completed gets checkmark. Connectors are thin vertical lines. FAIL/PASS arrows flash between critic/builder. Pro: matches existing pages, easy to read, great for presentations. Con: less visually dramatic than SVG.

Approach C — Timeline Swimlane: Horizontal timeline with agent lanes. Events appear as blocks on their agent's lane. Pro: shows parallelism, temporal relationships. Con: doesn't match existing design system, complex scrolling, overwhelming for demos.

Recommendation: Approach B. The vertical stacked box pattern already exists, is proven readable, and the adversarial loop section with its dashed red border is already the visual centrepiece of build-pipeline.html. Pair it with a dark terminal console on the left for the 'engineering dashboard' aesthetic.
Orchestrator active

Ideator recommends Approach B (DOM vertical boxes) — matches existing design system and is presentation-friendly. Explorer confirms the pattern already exists. Need researcher to validate: can we do smooth playback with vanilla JS? Any gotchas with GitHub Pages static hosting?

events.question: The ideator generated 3 approaches. The recommendation is Approach B. Which do you want to proceed with?

options.label: Approach B — DOM boxes (recommended)
options.description: Vertical stacked boxes + dark terminal console. Split-screen engineering dashboard. Matches existing build-pipeline.html pattern.

options.label: Approach A — SVG animated diagram
options.description: Animated SVG with stroke-dashoffset arrow animations. Crisp at any zoom but complex layout math.

options.label: Explore further before deciding
options.description: More questions to refine the direction.

events.text: > Go with B — the stacked boxes already look great on the overview page. Love the terminal console idea too. Definitely want the adversarial loop to be visually dramatic.
User chose Approach B. Dispatching researcher to validate technology choices.

events.args: agent=researcher, task="Validate: (1) Can vanilla JS handle smooth chained-setTimeout playback for 200+ events? (2) stroke-dashoffset for arrow animations — browser support? (3) GitHub Pages limitations for static HTML with fetch() for JSON logs? (4) Any lightweight alternatives to consider vs pure vanilla JS?"

events.text: Researcher active

events.args: vanilla JS setTimeout chain animation performance 2024

events.text: setTimeout chains are standard for sequential animation. requestAnimationFrame for visual updates. No framework needed for <1000 events. MDN confirms chained setTimeout is the correct pattern for variable-delay sequences.

events.args: stroke-dashoffset CSS animation browser support caniuse

events.text: stroke-dashoffset animation: 97% global support. Works in all modern browsers. Already used in 3 existing pages in this project (traceArrow keyframe).

events.args: GitHub Pages fetch JSON same-origin static site

events.text: GitHub Pages serves static files with correct MIME types. fetch() works for same-origin JSON files. No CORS issues for files in the same repo. URL params (?logid=) work fine — no server-side routing needed.

All validated. Vanilla JS + chained setTimeout is the correct approach — no framework needed. stroke-dashoffset has 97% browser support and is already used in this project. GitHub Pages handles fetch() for same-origin JSON with no issues. Alpine.js/Petite Vue considered but rejected — this is an imperative animation engine, not a reactive binding problem.
Orchestrator active
Technology validated. Dispatching architect to write the full implementation plan.

events.args: agent=architect, task="Design the Agent Flow Demo Visualiser. Single HTML file at docs/public/visualiser.html. Two-panel layout: console simulator (55%) + DOM vertical flow diagram (45%). 4 JS modules in IIFE: LogLoader, FlowController, ConsoleRenderer, PlaybackEngine. Write full plan with acceptance criteria, technical approach, edge cases."

events.text: Architect active

Key design decisions:
1. Single HTML file — matches all existing pages, no build step
2. Log format: JSON with agents[], transitions[], events[] — fully configurable per log
3. Phase-aware rendering: flow panel rebuilds per /plan and /build phases
4. Console panel: dark terminal aesthetic, distinct styling per event kind
5. Playback: chained setTimeout, seekTo() reconstructs state, keyboard shortcuts
6. FAIL/PASS arrows hidden by default, flash on transitions — dramatic for demos
7. Auto-scroll with user-override detection (wheel/touchmove, not scroll event)

Plan written: 170 lines. Sections: What it must do (17 items), What it must NOT do (12 constraints), Acceptance criteria (19 items), Technical approach (architecture, CSS, HTML structure, demo log spec, converter spec, execution order), Research validation table, Edge cases (12 scenarios).

Plan complete. Key deliverables: visualiser.html (4 JS modules, ~1000 lines), 2 demo log JSON files, converter.py (Python 3 stdlib). Architecture: LogLoader → FlowController + ConsoleRenderer driven by PlaybackEngine. Phase-aware flow panel, auto-scroll console, FAIL/PASS arrow animations, full scrubber with seekTo state reconstruction.
Orchestrator active

events.args: git add plans/2026-04-06-2012-agent-flow-demo-visualiser.md && git commit -m "Add plan: Agent-Flow Demo Visualiser"
